message:
chore(fnp-ufunc,fnp-conformance): diagnostic expect() messages for 43 gated unwraps (br-franken_numpy-5hef, br-franken_numpy-7qp9)

Replace 43 .unwrap() calls sitting on statically-guaranteed invariants with
.expect("...") so a future refactor that breaks the invariant surfaces a
readable panic message instead of a bare Option/Result::unwrap panic. No
semantic or behavioral change; audit-origin: audit_numpy_mocks.md.

fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs (1 site):
  - line 17893 einsum split_once("...") — upstream contains("...") gate
    makes the split infallible; expect() now carries that invariant.

fnp-conformance/src/lib.rs (25 sites):
  - 24 polynomial-family fixtures: UFuncArray::new(vec![v.len()], v, DType::F64)
    where v is an owned Vec<f64> from cheb/herm/lag/leg add/sub/mul/div/pow
  - 1 scalar fixture at poly_family_val: UFuncArray::new(vec![1], vec![x], DType::F64)

fnp-conformance/src/bin/dump_expected.rs (17 sites):
  - 11 Generator::from_pcg64_dxsm(seed) where seed = 12345 (compile-time constant)
  - 6 distribution calls (dirichlet, noncentral_chisquare, noncentral_f, zipf,
    hypergeometric, multivariate_hypergeometric) with constant parameters

Build verified via rch exec -- cargo check -p fnp-ufunc -p fnp-conformance
--all-targets (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc): finished
in 56.1s, 0 errors, 1 pre-existing warning at lib.rs:20473 (unrelated,
iter_rng.next_u64 discard).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
